title: Composition, Accordion, Font Weight, Cursor & Snap
description: Five live UI demos: animation-composition stacking, :has() grid accordion, scroll-driven font weight, shape-morphing cursor, scrollsnapchange carousel.

drops.title: animation-composition: add
drops.description: Stack multiple @keyframes on the same transform — they all add together instead of the last one winning.
drops.concept: CSS animation-composition: add lets you layer multiple @keyframes animations that each modify the same property, with each one adding onto the previous rather than replacing it. This enables complex multi-layer motion from simple, composable keyframe blocks — a pattern emerging across Apple-style UI.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers · CSS animation-composition explainer

interaction.click: switch modes between separate / replace / add

drops.title: :has() Horizontal Accordion
drops.description: CSS-only grid where hovering one card expands it and compresses siblings via :has() sibling targeting.
drops.concept: By targeting the parent with :has(.card:nth-child(N):hover), CSS can rewrite grid-template-columns to give the hovered card 3x more space and compress the others. Zero JavaScript — just a state promoted by :has() and animated by CSS grid column transitions.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Variable Font Weight
drops.description: Headings grow from hairline (100) to ultra-black (900) as they scroll into view — pure CSS, zero JS.
drops.concept: Using animation-timeline: view() combined with @keyframes on font-variation-settings, text weight tracks exact scroll position. The animation runs off the main thread, making it silky smooth. animation-range: entry controls the exact scroll window where the fattening happens.
drops.source: Josh W. Comeau · scroll-driven-animations.style · MDN Web Docs

drops.title: Shape-Morphing Custom Cursor
drops.description: Custom cursor transforms shape based on semantic element type — ring, I-beam, spinning circle, crosshair, blink caret.
drops.concept: A div that follows the mouse via JS is styled with CSS transitions. On each mousemove, JS detects the semantic tag (a, button, p, img, input, draggable) and applies a CSS class that transforms the cursor's width, height, border-radius, and animation — all smoothly transitioned.

interaction.mouse: move over zones to see cursor morph

drops.title: scrollsnapchange Reactive Carousel
drops.description: Snap carousel that uses the new scrollsnapchange event to trigger spring entrance animations on the focused card.
drops.concept: The scrollsnapchange event fires on the scroll container when the snapped element changes — exactly once per snap, not continuously. This makes it ideal for triggering entrance animations, updating indicators, and syncing UI state. Falls back gracefully to IntersectionObserver on older browsers.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers · scroll snap events · Chrome 129+
